revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:弁才天, en:Benzaiten); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/96506089 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: ja:Talk:弁才天 | revid=96506089 --> {{削除依頼ログ |talk=false |result1=削除|fullpage1=ノート:弁財天/削除|date1=2004年9月27日 }} ようやく「特定の版の削除」が終わりましたので、あらためてページ名の移動を告知したいと思います。現状、メインのページ名が「弁財天」で、リダイレクトが「弁才天」になっていますが、経典等に見える本来の尊名は「弁才天」であり、「財」字を用いる「弁財天」という表記は後世のもので、この点は議論の余地がありません。現代においても「弁才天」の表記は普通に使われており、メインのページ名を「弁才天」、リダイレクトを「弁財天」とするのが妥当と存じます。[[利用者:Urania|Urania]] 2005年5月18日 (水) 17:21 (UTC)
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1206250988 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Benzaiten | revid=1206250988 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|myth=yes|shinto=yes|religion=y}} {{WikiProject Women's History|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion}} {{WikiProject Women in Religion|importance=mid}} }}
Is calling Benzaiten a "Buddhist" goddess in the opening paragraph really the best choice. The Seven Lucky Gods - by which most Japanese know her - exist outside of mainstream Buddhism. This is certainly to be expected because some of the Seven Lucky Gods are actually Daoist, not Buddhist, and it is very unclear where Ebisu comes from, appearing in neither Buddhism nor Daoism. Benzaiten is worshipped in a distinctly different fashion from the most famous Buddhist goddess in Japan - Kannon. [[User:Jrhoadley|Jrhoadley]] ([[User talk:Jrhoadley|talk]]) 19:10, 1 December 2016 (UTC)
== Benten in popular culture ==
She is a recurring Character in the japanese manga and animation series [[Urusei Yatsura]], by [[Takahashi Rumiko]]. She is a childhood friend of the main character Lum. In the series she is pictured as biker with a flying motor cycle. And she is the leader of a gang of female bikers, that comprises of the daughers of the other lucky gods. <!-- Template:Unsigned IP --><small class="autosigned">— Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/133.56.199.80|133.56.199.80]] ([[User talk:133.56.199.80#top|talk]]) 03:04, 29 November 2019 (UTC)</small> <!--Autosigned by SineBot-->
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Who said Benzaiten originally formed from Hindu god Saraswati? ==
We all know that the Mahayana branch of Buddhism worships various gods and goddesses. And in ancient India Emperor Kanishka who was a Buddhist king originally developed such beautiful art. Those who claim to be Hindus There is no evidence of where their idols came from and who made them, but they stole many Buddhist idols.When Buddhism almost disappeared from India. Today Buddhist remains can be found in many Hindu temples। And I am not claiming that it is a Buddhist goddess but the real history should be known by all.'''Bold''' [[Special:Contributions/103.127.0.145|103.127.0.145]] ([[User talk:103.127.0.145|talk]]) 17:39, 3 June 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/96506089 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: ja:Talk:弁才天 | revid=96506089 --> {{削除依頼ログ |talk=false |result1=削除|fullpage1=ノート:弁財天/削除|date1=2004年9月27日 }} ようやく「特定の版の削除」が終わりましたので、あらためてページ名の移動を告知したいと思います。現状、メインのページ名が「弁財天」で、リダイレクトが「弁才天」になっていますが、経典等に見える本来の尊名は「弁才天」であり、「財」字を用いる「弁財天」という表記は後世のもので、この点は議論の余地がありません。現代においても「弁才天」の表記は普通に使われており、メインのページ名を「弁才天」、リダイレクトを「弁財天」とするのが妥当と存じます。[[User:Urania|Urania]] 2005年5月18日 (水) 17:21 (UTC)
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1206250988 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Benzaiten | revid=1206250988 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|myth=yes|shinto=yes|religion=y}} {{WikiProject Women's History|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion}} {{WikiProject Women in Religion|importance=mid}} }}
Is calling Benzaiten a "Buddhist" goddess in the opening paragraph really the best choice. The Seven Lucky Gods - by which most Japanese know her - exist outside of mainstream Buddhism. This is certainly to be expected because some of the Seven Lucky Gods are actually Daoist, not Buddhist, and it is very unclear where Ebisu comes from, appearing in neither Buddhism nor Daoism. Benzaiten is worshipped in a distinctly different fashion from the most famous Buddhist goddess in Japan - Kannon. [[User:Jrhoadley|Jrhoadley]] ([[User talk:Jrhoadley|talk]]) 19:10, 1 December 2016 (UTC)
== Benten in popular culture ==
She is a recurring Character in the japanese manga and animation series [[Urusei Yatsura]], by [[Takahashi Rumiko]]. She is a childhood friend of the main character Lum. In the series she is pictured as biker with a flying motor cycle. And she is the leader of a gang of female bikers, that comprises of the daughers of the other lucky gods. <!-- Template:Unsigned IP --><small class="autosigned">— Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/133.56.199.80|133.56.199.80]] ([[User talk:133.56.199.80#top|talk]]) 03:04, 29 November 2019 (UTC)</small> <!--Autosigned by SineBot-->
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Who said Benzaiten originally formed from Hindu god Saraswati? ==
We all know that the Mahayana branch of Buddhism worships various gods and goddesses. And in ancient India Emperor Kanishka who was a Buddhist king originally developed such beautiful art. Those who claim to be Hindus There is no evidence of where their idols came from and who made them, but they stole many Buddhist idols.When Buddhism almost disappeared from India. Today Buddhist remains can be found in many Hindu temples। And I am not claiming that it is a Buddhist goddess but the real history should be known by all.'''Bold''' [[Special:Contributions/103.127.0.145|103.127.0.145]] ([[User talk:103.127.0.145|talk]]) 17:39, 3 June 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Benzaiten here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Benzaiten for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/96506089 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: ja:Talk:弁才天 | revid=96506089 --> {{削除依頼ログ |talk=false |result1=削除|fullpage1=ノート:弁財天/削除|date1=2004年9月27日 }} ようやく「特定の版の削除」が終わりましたので、あらためてページ名の移動を告知したいと思います。現状、メインのページ名が「弁財天」で、リダイレクトが「弁才天」になっていますが、経典等に見える本来の尊名は「弁才天」であり、「財」字を用いる「弁財天」という表記は後世のもので、この点は議論の余地がありません。現代においても「弁才天」の表記は普通に使われており、メインのページ名を「弁才天」、リダイレクトを「弁財天」とするのが妥当と存じます。[[User:Urania|Urania]] 2005年5月18日 (水) 17:21 (UTC)
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1206250988 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Benzaiten | revid=1206250988 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|myth=yes|shinto=yes|religion=y}} {{WikiProject Women's History|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion}} {{WikiProject Women in Religion|importance=mid}} }}
Is calling Benzaiten a "Buddhist" goddess in the opening paragraph really the best choice. The Seven Lucky Gods - by which most Japanese know her - exist outside of mainstream Buddhism. This is certainly to be expected because some of the Seven Lucky Gods are actually Daoist, not Buddhist, and it is very unclear where Ebisu comes from, appearing in neither Buddhism nor Daoism. Benzaiten is worshipped in a distinctly different fashion from the most famous Buddhist goddess in Japan - Kannon. [[User:Jrhoadley|Jrhoadley]] ([[User talk:Jrhoadley|talk]]) 19:10, 1 December 2016 (UTC)
== Benten in popular culture ==
She is a recurring Character in the japanese manga and animation series [[Urusei Yatsura]], by [[Takahashi Rumiko]]. She is a childhood friend of the main character Lum. In the series she is pictured as biker with a flying motor cycle. And she is the leader of a gang of female bikers, that comprises of the daughers of the other lucky gods. <!-- Template:Unsigned IP --><small class="autosigned">— Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/133.56.199.80|133.56.199.80]] ([[User talk:133.56.199.80#top|talk]]) 03:04, 29 November 2019 (UTC)</small> <!--Autosigned by SineBot-->
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Who said Benzaiten originally formed from Hindu god Saraswati? ==
We all know that the Mahayana branch of Buddhism worships various gods and goddesses. And in ancient India Emperor Kanishka who was a Buddhist king originally developed such beautiful art. Those who claim to be Hindus There is no evidence of where their idols came from and who made them, but they stole many Buddhist idols.When Buddhism almost disappeared from India. Today Buddhist remains can be found in many Hindu temples। And I am not claiming that it is a Buddhist goddess but the real history should be known by all.'''Bold''' [[Special:Contributions/103.127.0.145|103.127.0.145]] ([[User talk:103.127.0.145|talk]]) 17:39, 3 June 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:54, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
